claimBoundary: Passing this matrix verifies only the automatable WCAG 2.2 AA slice on the tested host target. It does not certify TalkBack, VoiceOver, Narrator, or Orca output, physical touch/keyboard usability, packaged OS behavior, or full WCAG 2.2 AA conformance. No platform is Full support; every manual follow-up below is REQUIRED and NOT RUN.

claimPolicy.blocked: At least one required dimension is unavailable with no tested alternative path.
claimPolicy.degraded: Every applicable dimension is verified but at least one carries a declared, visible degradation.
claimPolicy.full: Every applicable dimension is automated-verified and has no unresolved device/AT/OS follow-up.
claimPolicy.unverified: At least one applicable dimension lacks platform/AT evidence; Full is prohibited.

A11Y-CONTRAST.summary: Text contrast is asserted for representative surfaces via textContrastGuideline; forced-colors/high-contrast rendering remains a manual follow-up.

A11Y-DESKTOP-MENU-SHORTCUTS.summary: Desktop menu bar and command palette expose discoverable, keyboard-operable shortcuts driving the same routes.

A11Y-KEYBOARD-OPERABILITY.summary: Primary actions are reachable and activatable from the keyboard (focus + LogicalKeyboardKey activation), not pointer-only.

A11Y-NO-DRAG-ONLY.summary: Reorder and other pointer gestures always have a discrete, named, keyboard-operable alternative (Move up/down), never drag-only.

A11Y-REDUCED-MOTION.summary: Reduced-motion (disableAnimations) removes non-essential animation; state changes still complete with a non-motion cue.

A11Y-SEMANTICS-NAME-ROLE-STATE.summary: Interactive controls expose accessible name/role/state via Semantics; screens are asserted with the semantics tree enabled and labelled-tap-target guideline.

A11Y-TARGET-SIZE.summary: Primary touch controls meet the 48x48 dp minimum via androidTapTargetGuideline across representative screens.

A11Y-TEXT-SCALE.summary: Core screens remain usable without layout overflow at 200% text (TextScaler.linear(2)).

manualEvidence.MANUAL-A11Y-ATSPI-CLAIMED-001: Each exact Linux distribution + desktop + package + supported AT-SPI combination in this matrix: Orca traversal, accessible-tree inspection, announcements, native dialogs, high contrast, lock, backup, and recovery. No generic 'Linux passed' result is acceptable.
manualEvidence.MANUAL-A11Y-BACKGROUND-DESKTOP-PACKAGED-: Packaged Windows and each claimed Linux combination: background/resume reconciliation and accessible reminder-diagnostics surface after wake.
manualEvidence.MANUAL-A11Y-BACKGROUND-MOBILE-PHYSICAL-0: Android and supported iOS physical devices: opportunistic background reconciliation under Doze/App Standby/BackgroundTasks; correctness must never depend on background firing, and reminder diagnostics must stay accessible.
manualEvidence.MANUAL-A11Y-FORCED-CONTRAST-001: Windows High Contrast and claimed Linux high/forced-contrast behavior plus supported mobile contrast settings: boundaries, states, custom painting, charts, focus, and scrims.
manualEvidence.MANUAL-A11Y-MAGNIFICATION-001: Representative mobile and desktop magnification/display zoom: focus follows viewport; dialogs, menus, errors, and recovery copy remain reachable.
manualEvidence.MANUAL-A11Y-NARRATOR-PACKAGED-001: Packaged Windows target on representative hardware: Narrator scan/read/control modes, keyboard-only traversal, menus/shortcuts, splitter, file-picker return, app lock, backup, Recovery Mode, high contrast, and 200% text.
manualEvidence.MANUAL-A11Y-NOTIFY-ANDROID-PHYSICAL-001: Android physical devices across supported API levels: runtime notification permission, exact-alarm absent/present, Doze/battery restriction, reboot re-arm, representative OEM background limits, and notification action delivery/redaction accessibility.
manualEvidence.MANUAL-A11Y-NOTIFY-IOS-PHYSICAL-001: Supported iOS physical device: permission states, pending-request quota reconciliation, terminated/background delivery, timezone change, and notification action accessibility.
manualEvidence.MANUAL-A11Y-NOTIFY-LINUX-CLAIMED-001: Each claimed Linux distribution + desktop: freedesktop notification daemon presence, action support, and Orca-announced notification accessibility.
manualEvidence.MANUAL-A11Y-NOTIFY-WINDOWS-PACKAGED-001: Packaged Windows target with registered package identity: toast scheduling/actions, Action Center accessibility with Narrator, and timezone reconciliation.
manualEvidence.MANUAL-A11Y-PRIVACY-SURFACES-001: Physical/mobile and packaged desktop checks of notification, widget, app-switcher/recent-app, window preview, background/resume, restoration, and Recovery Mode redaction in every lock state.
manualEvidence.MANUAL-A11Y-SWITCH-ANDROID-PHYSICAL-001: Android physical device with platform switch access: sequential scan order, activation, menus, reorder, recurrence choices, editor toolbar/body, and recovery actions.
manualEvidence.MANUAL-A11Y-SWITCH-DESKTOP-001: Windows and every claimed Linux combination using switch-equivalent keyboard/sequential input: no pointer-only, drag-only, hover-only, right-click-only, or double-click-only command.
manualEvidence.MANUAL-A11Y-SWITCH-IOS-PHYSICAL-001: Supported iOS physical device with Switch Control: sequential/group scan, activation, escape/back, editor and destructive confirmation paths.
manualEvidence.MANUAL-A11Y-TALKBACK-PHYSICAL-001: Android physical representative device(s): TalkBack read/swipe/activate, Explore by Touch, hardware keyboard where supported, focus recovery, live regions, lock/privacy, and 200% text.
manualEvidence.MANUAL-A11Y-VOICEOVER-PHYSICAL-001: Supported iOS physical representative device(s) when the V1 claim applies: VoiceOver rotor/read/activate, hardware keyboard, focus recovery, live regions, widgets/lock privacy, and 200% text.
manualEvidence.MANUAL-A11Y-WIDGET-ANDROID-PHYSICAL-001: Android physical device: home-screen widget sizes, locked-surface privacy redaction, stale-snapshot state, deep links, and committed widget actions with TalkBack/switch access.
manualEvidence.MANUAL-A11Y-WIDGET-IOS-PHYSICAL-001: Supported iOS physical device: WidgetKit sizes, locked-surface privacy, stale snapshot, deep links, and committed actions with VoiceOver/Switch Control.

statePolicy.automated-verified: The automatable slice of this dimension is covered by real in-repo tests that pass on the tested host target. Any experiential slice (real assistive technology, hardware keyboard, platform conventions) is named as a manual follow-up.
statePolicy.manual-required: This dimension cannot be established in-repo. It is entirely gated on a physical device, real assistive technology, or a real OS/package, and every follow-up is enumerated as a MANUAL-* item.
statePolicy.not-applicable: This dimension does not exist on this platform (for example, mobile home-screen widgets on desktop).

assistiveTechnology.declaredDegradation: TalkBack and Switch Access output is platform-generated and can differ from the Flutter semantics tree; it cannot be produced in-repo.

background.declaredDegradation: BackgroundScheduler.configure() may return unavailable/permissionDenied; correctness never depends on a background callback firing (design non-goal). Physical Doze/App-Standby behavior is device-gated.

notification.declaredDegradation: Until physical-device evidence exists the app treats Android notifications as available-but-flagged: SchedulerCapability reports actionsSupported=false and exactAlarms=false while the scheduling matrix stays unverified, so reminders schedule best-effort and surface honest ReminderDiagnostic codes instead of silently failing.

widget.declaredDegradation: ForgeWidgetBridge returns FailureKind.unavailableCapability on unhandled/timed-out actions; snapshot redaction and locked-surface privacy require the native home-screen widget host on a device.

assistiveTechnology.declaredDegradation: VoiceOver and Switch Control output is platform-generated; iOS is build/spike-only until the V1 support gate, so no MVP inference follows from a build.

background.declaredDegradation: BackgroundTasks opportunism is best-effort; correctness never depends on it. Tests SHALL never assert a fixed background frequency.

notification.declaredDegradation: iOS notifications degrade to best-effort with a bounded pending-request quota; SchedulerCapability actionsSupported/exactAlarms stay false until device evidence exists. Never asserts a fixed background frequency.

widget.declaredDegradation: WidgetKit snapshots and shared-container behavior require a signed build on a device; the bridge fails closed with unavailableCapability off-device.

assistiveTechnology.declaredDegradation: Orca/AT-SPI output is generated by the desktop stack and must be verified on each exact claimed distribution + desktop + package combination.

background.declaredDegradation: Background/resume reconciliation is opportunistic; correctness never depends on it. Wake behavior needs each claimed packaged desktop combination.

notification.declaredDegradation: freedesktop notification daemon presence and action support vary by distribution/desktop; untested combinations are unsupported, never inferred from a nearby result.

widget.declaredDegradation: Linux has no mobile home-screen widget surface; desktop companion windows are Post-V1 and out of scope for this matrix.

assistiveTechnology.declaredDegradation: Narrator scan/read/control-mode output is generated by Windows and requires a packaged build on representative hardware.

background.declaredDegradation: Background/resume reconciliation is opportunistic; correctness never depends on it. Wake behavior needs a packaged desktop build.

notification.declaredDegradation: Windows toast scheduling/actions and timezone reconciliation require a packaged build with registered package identity; the scheduling matrix stays unverified until that exists.

widget.declaredDegradation: Windows has no mobile home-screen widget surface; desktop companion windows are Post-V1 and out of scope for this matrix.
